Audit Skips:

/home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-9vx7k_u9/PKGBUILD: [SKIPPED] Skipping binary file: PKGBUILD

data.reasoning: Both models failed to actually review any files. The PKGBUILD — the single most critical file for AUR safety since it controls what executes during makepkg — was incorrectly classified as a 'binary file' and skipped. No content was analyzed, so no safety determination can be made. A verdict of inconclusive is the only defensible outcome.
data.disagreement_analysis: There is no substantive disagreement. Both models produced identical 'skipped' results due to the same tooling bug (PKGBUILD misclassified as binary). The agreement is on a non-result, not on a safety verdict.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD was never reviewed by either model — it was erroneously flagged as a binary file and skipped
Zero files reviewed total; no .install hooks, source files, or build scripts were examined
The initial PKGBUILD safety gate failed, which prevented any deeper file traversal
false_positives: Classification of PKGBUILD as a 'binary file' is a tooling false positive — PKGBUILD files are plain-text shell scripts and should always be readable as text
re_audit_focus: PKGBUILD (force text decoding; inspect prepare/build/check/package functions and any source URLs)
Source array entries and pkgver() logic for network/filesystem access
learnings: The binary-file detection heuristic is broken for PKGBUILD: PKGBUILDs must be exempt from binary detection (whitelist by filename) since they are always shell text and are the highest-priority file to audit

When the initial PKGBUILD audit gate fails due to a file-read/classification error (as opposed to a genuine malicious finding), the tool should retry with forced text decoding rather than aborting the entire audit
A 'skipped' result triggered by a parsing/encoding error should be surfaced as a tooling failure distinct from a security-motivated skip, to avoid masking unreviewed packages
Encoding detection likely tripped on a BOM, non-UTF-8 bytes, or null bytes in the PKGBUILD; consider latin-1/UTF-8 fallback decoding for known text filetypes
Both models hit the identical failure, indicating the issue is in the harness/file-loading layer, not model reasoning — fixing the file selector will unblock all models simultaneously

data.reasoning: Both models incorrectly skipped PKGBUILD as a binary file, preventing any meaningful audit. PKGBUILD is critical for assessing build/install behavior.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD skipped entirely by both models
No review of install hooks or build scripts
false_negatives: Potential risks in PKGBUILD (build commands, source validation)
Untrusted source handling in package functions
learnings: Fix binary file detection: PKGBUILD should never be skipped as binary
Prioritize PKGBUILD/.install files even if other files are skipped
Add fallback mechanism when critical files are misclassified
Improve pre-audit checks to validate file types before processing

data.reasoning: Both models incorrectly skipped PKGBUILD as a binary file, preventing any meaningful audit. PKGBUILD is critical for assessing build/install behavior.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD skipped entirely by both models

false_negatives: Potential risks in PKGBUILD (build commands, source validation)

Prioritize PKGBUILD/.install files even if other files are skipped
